narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after pouring so much into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as riskier than we'd hope—landing at a 65% chance of not passing. Picture your journey like a story the model pieced together from key chapters of your background, and the biggest one was your Year 3 decile of 2, which stood out as the strongest signal pulling toward struggle, suggesting those later grades didn't climb as high as they often do for those who make it through. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 26, which the model flagged as another heavy weight tipping the scales toward failure, even though your undergraduate GPA of 3.5 showed real promise early on. Your Year 1 decile of 1 added to that early narrative of challenge, painting a picture of hurdles from the start. Then there's your middle family income bracket, which the model linked to added pressures that sometimes make the bar tougher to conquer. Your Asian background played a role too, as did being female, both nudging the story toward higher risk in patterns we've seen before. Even planning to go full-time didn't quite offset these, and while your target law interest is clear, it couldn't rewrite the overall tale. It's like these pieces together formed a profile that, sadly, mirrors too many who hit roadblocks at the bar. This isn't about your worth—it's the model's honest read on the odds based on what similar stories have shown. If you boost that LSAT or push your deciles higher next time, you could flip this script entirely. Hang in there; your determination shines through, and with targeted steps, the next chapter could be yours.
